exercises.description: Obvious derivative of the Robert Plant...At a stadium, wide stairwell or amphitheater, start with 1 Burpee at bottom, 1 incline push-up on first step, run to top, 1 squat on top step, then run back down. Repeat 7 times up increasing reps of each exercise by 1 each time, so you finish with 7 burpees, 7 incline push-ups, run to top, 7 squats at top. Other exercises ascending from 1-7 can be added or substituted, such as supine pull-ups if you have the apparatus available, like a stadium railing.

exercises.name: Star Jack Burpee
exercises.description: A burpee variation that includes a StarJack instead of the jump at the end. The StarJack is performed by jumping and raising your arms and legs out to your sides, so your body forms an ''X'' in mid-air.

exercises.name: Starfish Crunch
exercises.description: The PAX lay spread eagle on their backs, feet just above ground. On count of 1, the PAX crunch at the core bringing the left leg and right arm together, On 2, return to start. On 3, crunch bringing the right leg and left arm together, on 4, return to start. Rinse and repeat many, many times.

exercises.name: Steel Blades
exercises.description: The evil twin of Blades of Steel - Laying flat on your back, push your upper body up using your elbows. If you are super BA, you push you six off the ground so you are in a reverse plank on your heels and elbows.

exercises.name: Step into the Circle
exercises.description: Performed to the song Move by Thousand Foot Krutch, Pax plank up in a circle. Every time the song says move take one plank walk to the left then to the right the next time it says move. Whenever it says Circle the Pax spin in a circle. The progression goes: plank walk right one step, spin in a circle, plank walk left one step. For all the studs out there you can do merkins or plank jacks waiting on the verses, but for most 3 + minutes of plank-walking and spinning in circles is plenty. Great warm-up and will get the sweat dripping and the shoulders burning to start off the workout.

exercises.name: Step Ups
exercises.description: On a low wall, raise each leg one at a time up on the wall. Sometimes the second leg can be brought up to the chest before placing on wall. Often times done in a ladder along with another exercise.

exercises.name: Steve Earle
exercises.description: Starting position is standing w/hands behind your head. In four-count cadence, perform Copperhead Squat with a Hillbilly thrown in on the 4th count of the cadence.

exercises.name: Still C.A.L.V.E.S.
exercises.description: Play Dr. Dre's \Still D.R.E.\" Use a curb

exercises.name: Stink B.O.M.B.S.
exercises.description: B.O.M.B.S. x 2 on reps + use a spoke layout. Set 5 cones out surrounding the Shovel Flag. Pax #1 starts the B.O.M.B.S. reps at the first cone while pax #2 goes to the Shovel Flag to do exercise chosen by the Q (for example 50 curls or 50 LBC's). Pax #2 then swaps with Pax #1. Work at same spoke until all reps are completed. Then move to next spoke. Q can change center exercise with each move to next spoke. B.O.M.B.S = 100 Burpees, 200 Over Head Presses, 300 Merkins, 400 Big Boy Sit-ups, 500 Squats.

exercises.name: Stirkin
exercises.description: Steps ups, one each leg, then irkin using same surface as used for the stepups

exercises.name: Stump Huggers
exercises.description: Start out in the bear crawl position hold in a plank. Come down to elbow (plank) on left arm then right, then up to hands.

exercises.name: Sugar Bear Crawls
exercises.description: Modified bear crawl. In bear crawl position, legs stay straight and bounce side to side and propel the body forward.

exercises.name: Sugar Cookie
exercises.description: Works best on a baseball field with wet grass and a dirt infield. Do a called number of Supermans while kicking legs in cadence, as though you are swimming in the grass. Sprint to dirt infield and do called hand-release merkins followed by big boys. Rinse and repeat 3 rounds. Replicates a Navy punishment where you have to jump in the ocean followed by exercises on the beach. You get covered by sand and look like a sugar cookie.

exercises.name: Suicide Ladder
exercises.description: A shuttle run with an exercise set after each return to the starting line. After each full shuttle run the exercise count goes up by five reps. Repeat until out of time or until no one can take it anymore.

exercises.name: Suicide Squats
exercises.description: Start in normal starting squat position, squat, ~50% of regular squat, then back to normal starting position. Then go to full perpendicular regular proper squat, then back to normal vertical position, then finally go to full deep ankle squat before returning to vertical starting position. Q typically leads this exercise in a cadence of 1, 2, 3, or baby, regular, deep after which PAX reply with the rep count. Great towards the end of a warm-up to transition into the meat of the beatdown.

exercises.name: Suicides
exercises.description: Done on basketball court or football field. On basketball court, start at end line, sprint to foul line, touch the ground, sprint back to end line touch the ground, sprint to mid court and touch line, sprint back to end line and touch line, sprint to far foul line and touch ground, sprint back to end line and touch the ground, sprint to far end line and touch the ground, sprint back to original end line. Rinse and repeat.

exercises.name: Sumo Deadlift High Pull (SDHP)
exercises.description: Coupon exercise. Straddle your vertical coupon in sumo (wide; more than shoulder width apart) stance with feet pointed out. Keep your back straight. Cross your arms and grab the top of your coupon. Lift up and raise your elbows up to your eyes like deadlift. Lower arms until coupon hits the ground. Repeat.

exercises.name: Sumo Squat
exercises.description: Feet in wide stance (like a sumo wrestler), squat down and touch ground with both hands. For added fun can add sumo sounds and sumo leg lift between each rep. Also done by some groups as a jump squat with the same touch of the ground.

exercises.name: Super 21
exercises.description: Do 1 merkin followed by 1 situp; 2 merkins / 2 situps; 3 merkins / 3 situps, etc., etc. ....... up to 21 merkins/ 21 situps (total of 231 merkins and 231 situps). Insert other exercises in between to allow for recovery (i.e. - Super 21 #1 - #10, Monkey Humpers x 21, Super 21 #11-#13, Mountain Climbers x 21, Super 21 #14-#15, etc.).

exercises.name: Superman
exercises.description: Lie prone on the ground with arms, legs, and head held up as high as possible. Hold position to tolerance.

exercises.name: Superman Merkin
exercises.description: Plank starting position. Combine a merkin with the superman move. Go down, superman (release hands, feet and hands up off the ground, move hands from over your head to beside you with arms bent at an angle), hands back under you and push up.

exercises.name: Superman Yellow Sun (and Red Sun)
exercises.description: While in Superman position, perform sun gods. Yellow sun going forward, red sun going reverse. Usually done in cadence. Best when ground is wet or frozen to elicit maximum mumble chatter.

exercises.name: Supines
exercises.description: A sit-up performed from lying flat on your back with your knees flat on the ground. Sit up until you touch your toes.

exercises.name: Surfees
exercises.description: This name has been submitted twice: 1) Burpees in the surf, or 2) Similar to a burpee, but you jump up into the surf position with the majority of your weight on your back foot.

exercises.name: Surfer Merkin
exercises.description: Perform a 360-degree jump, then go down for a merkin, pop back up, and repeat.

exercises.name: Surftanamo
exercises.description: Surftanamo Similar to Guantanamo, but performed in a straight line along the surf, incorporating the ocean.

exercises.name: Suzanne Somers
exercises.description: Suzanne Somers Ode to the \Thigh Master\" infomercial from everyone''s favorite girl on Three''s Company : Perform a regular squat but point your toes outward. This is a great exercise for the inner thigh. Ensure good form by keeping your stance wide

exercises.name: Sweat Angels
exercises.description: Sweat Angels On your six, perform a double dolly (arms and legs 6 inches off the ground) as if making a snow angel.

exercises.name: Swerkins
exercises.description: Swerkins Pushup with feet in in a playground swing. As you complete the pushup, bring your knees into your chest and back out.

exercises.name: Swingset Orangutans
exercises.description: Jump up and grab the top of the swingset sideways, then walk hand over hand to the other end.

exercises.name: Swirley
exercises.description: PAX holds the other's legs while they perform push-ups in a handstand position (as if receiving a swirly).

exercises.name: T-Bomb
exercises.description: Start in a crab position, feet together under your 6, hands and feet on the ground. IC feet go: 1) Legs straight, feet together 2) Legs straight and spread 3) Feet back together and legs straight 4) Feet together and back under your 6 That is one rep. Don't let your 6 touch the ground!

exercises.name: TaMABA
exercises.description: A 4-minute Tabata routine, 8-rounds, 20-second on, 10-second off... 5 Burpees per round = 40 Burpees

exercises.name: Tammy Wynette
exercises.description: Tammy Wynette''s country song hit ''Stand By Your Man'' inspires this exercise. Starting Position: PAX 1 assumes the merkin plank while PAX 2 stands beside \his man.\" Exercise: PAX 1 performs merkins in sync with PAX 2
aliases: who does deep squats. Complete 10 reps either by Q call or on their own. At multiples of 10

exercises.name: Tempo Merkin
exercises.description: Regular Merkin, in cadence, with a 3-count down and a 1-count up.

exercises.name: Tennessee Iditarod
exercises.description: Tennessee Iditarod Like the Iditarod race but without snow, huskies, or sleds. Wrap a climbing rope around PAX One''s waist. PAX Two holds the rope, like reins, behind PAX One. PAX Two provides resistance while PAX One pulls \the sled.\" This works best with an AYG sprint and maximum resistance."

exercises.name: The 2020 Gobbler Challenge
exercises.description: 5 Rounds for Time F3 Standard Coupon or equivalent 10 Block Swings 10 reps of associated exercise 15 Block Swings 15 reps of associated exercise 25 Block Swings 25 reps of associated exercise 50 Block Swings 50 reps of associated exercise 400M Run Round 1: Hand Release Merkins Round 2: Body Weight Squats Round 3: Rows Round 4: Rowers Round 5: Overhead Press Total of 500 Swings and 100 reps of each exercise.

exercises.name: The 2021 Gobbler Challenge
exercises.description: 10 Rounds for Time 50M Bear Crawl 10 Rowers 50M Bear Crawl 20 Hand Release Merkins 50M Bear Crawl 10 Rowers 50M Bear Crawl 20 Squats Rinse and repeat for 10 rounds…

exercises.name: The 2022 Gobbler Challenge
exercises.description: 60-minute AMRAP F3 Standard Coupon or equivalent 400M Mosey after each round 10 T Merkins 20 Body Weight Squats 30 Rowers 40 Overhead Presses 50 Block Swings 60 Flutter Kicks (Block Over Head) 70 Rows 80 Lunges 90 Crunches Run 400M or Walk 200M

exercises.name: The 2023 Gobbler Challenge
exercises.description: 60-minute AMRAP F3 Standard Coupon or equivalent This year's workout is in honor of Gobbler’s father, who owned and operated his farm for 40 years. All exercises are inspired by farmwork. 100m Asymentric Farmer's Walk (Right) (Round)*10x Overhead Press w/ Coupon 100m Overhead Carry (Round)*10x Squats 100m Asymentric Farmer's Walk (Left) (Round)*10x Rowers 100m Overhead Carry (Round)*10x Block Swings

exercises.name: The 2024 Gobbler Challenge
exercises.description: 60-minute AMRAP F3 Standard Coupon or equivalent 50 Sitdown Overhead Presses Run 400M 50 Cactus Squats Run 400M 50 BBSU Run 400M 50 Block Swings Run 400M 50 SSH Run 400M Rinse and repeat until the time is up...

exercises.name: The 2025 Gobbler Challenge - “Ladder of Pain” AMRAP
exercises.description: 60-min Timer F3 Standard Coupon or equivalent 400M Mosey after each round 10 Overhead Squats +20 HR Merkins +30 Block Swings +40 Front Squats +50 OH Presses +60 Rowers +70 Sumo Squats +80 High Rows +90 LBCs +100 Lunges (2x1)

exercises.name: The Bruce and the Cait
exercises.description: This is a simple Merkin that incorporates elements of balance and core strength. For \The Bruce
aliases: \" start in a regular Merkin position with one leg crossed over the other at the ankle. When you come back up from the Merkin

exercises.name: The Butkus
exercises.description: The Butkus The exercise is named after Rocky''s dog and resembles the rapid toe tapping Rocky Balboa exercise at curbside. Use the curb as a step and alternate stepping as quickly as possible for one minute, then rest for one minute. Continue this pattern for another minute. Complete the sequence in THREE ROUNDS, hopefully without a TKO.

exercises.name: The Cooper
exercises.description: Run one lap around the track. Then perform 10 burpees, 10 squats, and 10 merkins. Run another lap, then do 9 burpees, 9 squats, and 9 merkins. Continue this pattern, counting down to 1. It gets pretty brutal when you reach around 5.

exercises.name: The Cycle
exercises.description: From home plate, bear crawl to 1st base, do 3 burpees, then bear crawl back to home plate. From home plate, bear crawl around the bases to 2nd base, do 6 burpees, then bear crawl back to home plate. From home plate, bear crawl around the bases to 3rd base, do 9 burpees, then bear crawl back to home plate. From home plate, bear crawl around the bases back to home plate.

exercises.name: The Dab
exercises.description: 10 burpees per minute for 5 minutes, then run 1/4 mile. Repeat this cycle 4 times.

exercises.name: The Dirty Dog
exercises.description: The Dirty Dog is a leg and core exercise. It is performed by being on all fours and executed one leg at a time. From the starting position the leg is raised to be parallel to the ground and then brought back to the start position. Proper form requires the leg to be fully parallel to the ground before returning to the starting position. The exercise is done one leg at a time. Pro Tip: the opposite leg can be moved to be more centered under your body to better support your weight and balance.

exercises.name: The Donatello
exercises.description: The Donatello To end a workout with suicides on a long set of stairs (e.g., Snake Pit''s Big Boy Stairs, which has 83 stairs and 7 landings).

exercises.name: The Doogie
exercises.description: It’s like murder bunny minus the coupon. It’s a 4 legged frog hop. It could also be called a “murder frog”. But I like the Doogie.

exercises.name: The Dumbledore
exercises.description: A Dumbledore is an exercise combination compiled of 1 Burpee, 1 Inch Worm, 4 Merkins (2 Shoulder Tap, 2 Knee Tap), 3 Squats.

exercises.name: The Elwood
exercises.description: The Elwood Like High Knees or Chopping Feet, but keep your arms in sync and symmetrical with your feet. When your right hand and right knee are up, your left hand and left knee are down, and vice versa. The PAX can settle into a steady driving quarter-note 4/4 rhythm with this. It''s a great way to bring in the six while keeping heart rates elevated.

exercises.name: The Grateful Dead (aka Dancing Bear)
exercises.description: Basically, it's a plank-o-rama with a bit of added silliness. Start with 5 Merkins. Rotate 360° in place to the left, raising your left arm, then right arm, and so on. Do 5 Werkins. Rotate 360° in place to the right, raising your left arm, then right arm, and so on. Next, perform 5 Diamond Merkins. While in place, lift your left arm and leg up, then quickly swap to your right arm and leg up. Continue this back and forth as quickly as you can, making sure to fully extend each limb. Now you look like a Dancing Bear!

exercises.name: The Gross
exercises.description: The Gross The beauty of this workout is that you can adjust the intensity based on the level of pain you want to inflict. Pick 12 exercises and perform 12 reps of each. That''s 12x12=144, which is known as a Gross. This workout was first done by Barry Manilow and YHC to showcase the exercises we hate the most, all in one session. It''s guaranteed to generate fantastic mumble chatter.

exercises.name: The Hightower
exercises.description: As the Nation knows, F3 Grand Strand lost Jacob \Hightower\" Hancher this past weekend while he was serving as a Myrtle Beach Police Officer. End of Watch: 10/03/20. We honored him with a full workout
aliases: and this is a part of it

exercises.name: The Hodor
exercises.description: The Hodor Inspired by Hodor from Game of Thrones and his sole purpose in life to carry a crippled boy. With a cinder block held in front of you, squat until the block touches the ground, stand, rotate the block over your head into a triceps extension, squat again, stand, and rotate the block back in front. That''s 1 rep. Repeat for 30 reps, followed by 1 lap around a 1/4 mile trackâ€''carrying the block.

exercises.name: The Incredible Murder Bunny
exercises.description: Mark a distance of 20 yards from a start line. To the tune of “Incredible” by Oh The Larceny, from the start line perform Murder Bunnies towards the 20 yard marker and once you reach the 20 yard point, perform a Redrum Bunnies back to the start line. During the exercise, anytime the word “incredible” is sung, step to the side of your coupon and perform a burpee. If you don’t finish on the start line, overhead carry your coupon back to the start line if needed when the song ends.

exercises.name: The Jeffrey Leonard
exercises.description: Done either on a baseball diamond or with cones set up like bases. PAX lines up single file at home plate and proceeds to sprint out a single, double, triple and home run with a jog back to home in between each sprint. Can be done as a quick warm up or dragged out to 1 single, 2 doubles, three triples and 4 home runs (done with flaps down of course).

exercises.name: The Killer Gnat
exercises.description: The Killer Gnat: A small exercise routine attracted to the lights in the park… Start with: 1 Burpee with 1 Merkin 1 Imperial Squat Walker, each with 1 Squat, 2x1 Motivators from 1 Mosey to the next light post and add 1 rep. . 2 Burpees, each with 2 Merkins 2 Imperial Squat Walkers, each with 2 Squats, 2x1 Motivators from 2 Mosey to the next light post and add 1 rep. . . . 10 Burpees, each with 10 Merkins 10 Imperial Squat Walkers, each with 10 Squats, 2x1 Motivators from 10 Options: Go to 5 and head back down to 1, or take it all the way to 10.

exercises.name: The King Louie
exercises.description: The King Louie is a 1:4 routine that consists of a Burpee followed by four Jungle Boy Squats. Ascending count to 10:40.

exercises.name: The Lion King
exercises.description: The Lion King Start with a coupon held tightly to your chest. Move into a low squat position, lowering the coupon between your knees. As you come up out of the squat, raise the coupon high above your head, thrusting it upward so that it becomes weightless for a fraction of a second. Think of Simba being presented in The Lion King .

exercises.name: The Maggie
exercises.description: Coupon chest press while doing flutter kicks.

exercises.name: The Man from LARAMIE
exercises.description: A stations/circuit workout created by the first Nantan at the Laramie, WY F3 location start, this workout contains seven stations, ideally located in a circle with about 5 yards (minimum) between stations. Mark the stations with cones. Using printed signage for the exercises at each station is helpful. PAX start at any station they desire, and complete the exercise at that station, then bear crawl or crab walk to the next station. Rotation is clockwise around the circle. The exercises are done AMRAP based on the Q's choice of duration. 60-90 seconds at each station with 10 seconds to rotate to next station works well for the first round. Rinse and Repeat as many times as desired, reducing the exercise time at each station for subsequent rounds, with an optional run loop around the larger area between rounds. The exercise stations each start with one of the letters in LARAMIE: L-unges; A-merican Hammers; R-everse Crunches; A-lternating Shoulder Taps; M-erkins; I-mperial Squat Walkers and E-leven Burpees. Q Tip: Using the Tabata app with a Bluetooth speaker to set the exercise timer and the \rest\" timer for the rotation makes the rotation rhythm smooth and allows the Q to not be overly encumbered with watching a timer and calling out start

exercises.name: The Oddjob
exercises.description: Was introduced to this fantastic warmup by a PAX member named Oddjob and started using it. Great for warmo's or just addition to The Thang. Being in a colder climate area this is a goto for me to get the body warmed up. 5 burpees | 10 SSH | 4 Burpees | 10 SSH | 3 Burpees | 10 SSH | 2 Burpees | 10 SSH | 1 Burpee | 20 SSH Burpees I usually do OYO and SSH are IC. Just wanted to share a fantastic 1-2 combo to get the blood pumping and have many sucking for air.

exercises.name: The Pete Hegseth Mile
exercises.description: Lace up and honor America’s ultimate fitness warrior, Pete Hegseth—our Secretary of War who’s all about ditching excuses, crushing complacency, and building unbreakable strength. This isn’t just any run; it’s a sub-7-minute mile that demands you push your limits like a true badass. Inspired by Hegseth’s no-nonsense ethos of peak physical readiness and zero tolerance for weakness, conquering this mile means channeling his veteran grit to outrun the ordinary. Achieve it, and know Pete would be proud—because in his world, fitness and being fat isn’t optional; it’s the foundation of victory. Go earn your badge

exercises.name: The Sheldon Cooper
exercises.description: Same as the Cooper just add big boy sit ups. Cooper = Run one lap around the track. Then perform 10 burpees, 10 squats, and 10 merkins. Run another lap, then do 9 burpees, 9 squats, and 9 merkins. Continue this pattern, counting down to 1.

exercises.name: The Sketch
exercises.description: Start face down raised up on hands and feet with knees bent. Pick up opposite hand and foot and pass foot under your body and through until you can touch your butt to the ground then back to where you started. That''s ''one''. Repeat on other side.

exercises.name: The Swimmer
exercises.description: Using a brick in each hand, the PAX performs the freestyle stroke.

exercises.name: The W.A.S.H.
exercises.description: The W.A.S.H. Werkins (50, preferably hand-release), American Hammers (100), Step-Ups (150), and Hip Thrusts (200). Perform these exercises either OYO (on your own) or in pairs. If done in pairs, one partner runsâ€''either laps or designated sprintsâ€''while the other completes the exercise. Switch as needed until all exercises are completed.

exercises.name: The Wifey
exercises.description: Pilates-inspired plank. Shift to one supporting arm, raising the other straight in the air. Switch and do the opposite side. Repeat as necessary.

exercises.name: The Wolverine
exercises.description: A big-boy burpee fit for a superhero: From a standing position, drop to plank, do one merkin, tuck your left knee to your chest, do another merkin, tuck your right knee to your chest, perform one more merkin, then stand, jump, and do an overhead clap. That's one rep. Rinse and repeat.

exercises.name: Theresa
exercises.description: Theresa The runner''s version of Mary, primarily consisting of stretching exercises led by the Q to promote optimal recovery and prevent injury after some intense street pounding.

exercises.name: Thigh Master
exercises.description: Thigh Master Starting position: Begin in a forward lunge with knees slightly bent. Perform the standard lunge movement by bending the back knee until it touches the ground, then return to the starting position. This is done in cadence: count 1 down, 2 up, 3 down, 4 up equals 1 rep. Continue for the set number of reps on the same leg, then switch legs and repeat. The exercise can be performed slowly (with the knee reaching the ground) or quickly, ultimately resulting in an intense \burn\" in the thighs."

exercises.name: Three Amigos
exercises.description: Three members of the PAX assume the air chair position, standing back-to-back-to-back and leaning on each other. Hold this position as you would with wall sits. You can either drop lower and hold or begin to walk as a group in the direction of your choosing. If the group fails to reach the destination, all three must do 10 burpees.

exercises.name: Thruster Mountain
exercises.description: A 45 minute mental + physical workout burner. Completed in 1 space the whole time. Used with a coupon or sandbag and a timer. 45 minute workout so be ready for a hot start and no Mary unless it is a 1 hour Black Diamond workout. The entire workout is thrusters. Make sure to use good form and keep back straight and go down to at least your knees with your elbows. Minute 1 = 1 Thruster. Minute 2 = 2 Thrusters. Minute 3 = 3 Thrusters Continue until Minute 23 (23 reps) which is the peak of the mountain. Minute 24 = 22 Thrusters and continue down the mountain each minute until you are back to 1 rep at the 45 minute mark.

exercises.name: Thrusters
exercises.description: Begin from a standing position with block. Move hips back and lower the torso to \sit\" as deeply as possible while maintaining an upright position. Return to a full standing position while also pressing arms upward to thrust block over your head. "

exercises.name: Thunderstruck
exercises.description: Thunderstruck The Q plays \Thunderstruck\" by AC/DC. PAX complete one standard merkin for every \"thunder!\" heard in the song and hold a plank during the rest of the song. It''s an awesome way to end a workout and ensure any remaining energy is fully burned away."

exercises.name: Till I Collapse
exercises.description: Play Eminem's \Till I Collapse\" Start in Plank . When Nate Dogg sings the hook (\"Till the roof comes off...\")

exercises.name: Tippin' Coupons Workout
exercises.description: A competitive team-based workout game played in 3-4 rounds of 5-7 minutes each, requiring ideally two coupons per PAX (minimum one per PAX). Count off by twos to split into two teams: Team 1 tries to end each round with the majority of coupons standing upright on their edge, while Team 2 tries to have them laying flat on their side. To flip a coupon to your team's position, you must first execute the round's prescribed Mode of Transportation (MOT) to reach any coupon, then 'pay' by completing the round's prescribed exercise before flipping it. Start each round with coupons distributed across the field, half upright and half laying down. Example rounds: Sprint + 5 Manmakers Sprint + 5 Murder Bunnies Bear Crawl + 20 American Hammers. This creates constant movement, competition, strategy, and camaraderie while providing a full-body workout

exercises.name: Tony Hawk Burpee
exercises.description: A normal burpee but when you jump, do a 360/180 degree spin in the air each time.

exercises.name: TONYA HARDING
exercises.description: Hand-release burpee combined with a single-leg vertical jump and an opposite-direction lateral skater hop. 2 sides = 1 complete rep. Execution: Descend into a burpee. Chest touches the ground. Hands fully release. Press to standing. Jump vertically off the right foot. Skater hop left. Land on left foot under control. Descend into next burpee. Jump vertically off the left foot. Skater hop right. Land on right foot under control. That is 1 complete rep. Standards: Chest must touch ground each rep. Hands must visibly release. Jump must be single-leg. Skater hop must be lateral and opposite direction of the jumping foot. Landing must be controlled before descending.

exercises.name: Tooth Fairy
exercises.description: Tooth Fairy With your feet at the bottom of a staircase (or other steep incline) and your hands on the 3rd or 4th step (depending on PAX height and step size), perform an incline Merkin. At the end of each Merkin, do a small hop with your hands and feet to the next steps (e.g., moving from steps 0 & 3 to steps 1 & 4). The longer the staircase, the better the burn. The name comes from PAX saying, \If you don''t do this right

exercises.name: Top Gun
exercises.description: Perform 10 reps each of 5 standing coupon exercises (e.g., Curls, Rows, Deadlifts, Overhead Press, Squats, Lunges) for 10 minutes. Repeat the 5 coupon exercises throughout the entire 10 minutes. Every minute on the minute, the Danger Zone occurs: PAX drop their coupons and perform 3 Burpees, then pick up where they left off with the coupon exercises. To increase the challenge, extend the time, increase the number of Burpees, or raise the reps of the exercises. Acapella versions of Kenny Loggins'' classic tune \Danger Zone\" are encouraged. "

exercises.name: Tortoise and the Hare B.O.M.B.S.
exercises.description: Buddy up with one rucksack per pair. Partner #1 puts on the rucksack and starts walking. Partner #2 performs the first exercise on the list and then chases down Partner #1. When Partner #2 catches up, they swap: Partner #2 takes the rucksack while Partner #1 does the same exercise. Repeat until each partner has completed all exercises 5 times. 5 Burpees (50 per pair) 10 Outlaws (100 per pair) 15 Merkins (150 per pair) 20 Big Boy Sit-ups (200 per pair) 25 Squats (250 per pair)

exercises.name: Tour de Humpers
exercises.description: Tour de Humpers PAX run around a neighborhood where multiple F3 members live (preferably more than three). At every stop sign, perform a combination of three exercises with the following counts: 10, 20, and 30 (e.g., 10 Merkins, 20 Carolina Dry-Docks, 30 Squats). When passing by a house where an F3 member lives, everyone stops in front of their house and performs 25 Monkey Humpers. This works best if the house has a Ring Doorbell. Invented by Aquaman to prevent fartsacking in an F3-heavy neighborhood in Cherokee.

exercises.name: Towel Circuit (Multiple Resistance Exercises)
exercises.description: Need small hand towels (or larger) and a partner. Circuit of different alternating push-pull dynamic resistance exercises that will work your body from top to bottom if done in this rotation. PAX 1 does the exercises to desired rep count with PAX 2 providing the resistance before switching roles. Shoulder Press (no towel) - PAX 1 sits down in squat rack shoulder press position with hands facing up. PAX 2 stands behind PAX 1 and presses down on hands providing resistance for PAX 1 while he pushes up like a shoulder press. Repeat to desired rep count. Curl (with towels) - PAX 1 Hold towel with palms up. PAX 2 links his towel over to interlock and goes to knee to provide resistance. PAX 1 does curls. Tricep Extension (with towels) PAX 1 holds towel out with palms down and puts it behind head like a skull crusher. PAX 2 stands behind PAX 1 and links towel and places 1 hand on back of PAX 1. Provide resistance while PAX 1 does tricep extensions (standing skull crushers). Merkins (with towel) - lay towel on ground. PAX 1 does Merkins until failure. Once PAX 1 hits failure point; PAX 2 staddles PAX 1 and grabs the towel while providing just enough resistance to help PAX 1 do 5 more Merkins beyond failure point. Seated Row (with towels) - PAX 1 & 2 sit down facing each other foot to foot with bent legs. PAX 1 holds towel with hands down. PAX 2 links towels and provides resistance. PAX 1 does rows pulling arms to chest. Foot Throwdowns (no towel) - PAX 1 lays down. PAX 2 stands with foot by PAX 1 shoulders while facing PAX 1. PAX 1 holds PAX 2 ankles and does leg lifts while PAX 2 throws down PAX 1 feet. PAX 1 stops the throwdown before his feet hit the ground and proceeds to do a leg lift before PAX 2 stops his feet and throws them down again.

exercises.name: Tower of Power
exercises.description: A challenging 9-count merkin variation using two upright coupons (cinderblocks) positioned slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Execute a sequence of nine push-ups while your hands 'climb' up and down the towers. Start with hands on the ground, perform merkins with hands progressively moving up the coupon (bottom, middle, top), then back down, creating a pyramid pattern. Descend as low as possible on each rep for full range of motion - anything less is a modification. This exercise builds upper body strength, core stability, and challenges you with varying hand positions and heights throughout the sequence, making it significantly harder than standard merkins

exercises.name: Toxic Run
exercises.description: Named after one of our Pax in the River City region who insists that jumping rope is the best way \to regulate your heart rate\". This exercise can be part of a warm up or as a cardio element to a beat down. The Q leads the Pax on a run with all Pax carrying a jump rope. At the Q's discretion
aliases: the Pax stop and do a number of jumps prescribed by the Q. Once all Pax have completed the required jumps

exercises.name: Traveling Side Straddle Hop
exercises.description: A @Side Straddle Hop while moving to a destination

exercises.name: Travolta Merkins
exercises.description: Travolta Merkins Picture Saturday Night Fever while doing Merkins. Start in plank position. The sequence is as follows: First move is a Merkin. 2nd move is right arm high. 3rd move is to reach under and across your body as far as you can. 4th move is to reach high again. 5th move is back to the Merkin. Flapjack with the left and then flapjack until you''re sick of singing \staying alive

exercises.name: Travoltas
exercises.description: Travoltas In the standard right/left arm-up plank position, lower the raised arm and reach as far as possible underneath and behind your body, then return it to the starting position. Think of the Stayin'' Alive move in the side plank position. Extra credit for using a pointed finger.

exercises.name: Triple Check
exercises.description: Teams of three: The first person holds a people's chair, the second person is in plank position in front of them, and the third person sprints 100 yards and back. Repeat this sequence three times.

exercises.name: Triple Nickel
exercises.description: Triple Nickel Find a hill. Start at the bottom, run to the top, and complete 5 reps of the called exercise. Run back to the bottom and complete 5 reps of the same exercise. This is known as a Triple Nickel (5/5/5). It can also be done on a flat surface with a launch point and a target point.

exercises.name: Triple Pump Squat Jumps
exercises.description: Perform a squat, then rise into a calf raise. Lower back down into the squat position, then come up halfway before returning to the squat position again. Finally, jump up to complete a squat jump.

exercises.name: Tunnel of Love
exercises.description: Tunnel of Love While PAX are planking side by side, one by one, someone crawls under the tunnel formed by the planking PAX. This exercise is best done with a ruck on, in the snow, or anywhere there is accumulated mud. Best done for a pre-determined distance like 25 yards. If enough PAX; can be a race between teams to reach the finish line.

exercises.name: Turk n Burp
exercises.description: A combination move of a Turkish Get Up and a Burpee: Perform a Turkish Get Up, then flip forward into the squat thrust of the Burpee. Follow with a push-up, complete the Burpee with a jump, and come back down to the ground to start your next rep with the Turkish Get Up. You really need to see it and find your own rhythm. Be the ball...

exercises.name: Turkish Get Up
exercises.description: Turkish Get Up Lying on your back, use only one arm and your legs to \get up.\""

exercises.name: Twerkin
exercises.description: Twerkin Start in the Downward Dog position, with your butt high in the air. Lower your butt slightly and rotate forward while performing the downward half of a Merkin, lowering your head and chest just above the ground as if going under a fence. Continue forward into the Upward Dog position, with your head up and arms extended for the upward half of the Merkin, hands slightly behind your shoulders and chest forward. Return to the starting position by lowering your head and chest, bending your arms, and rotating your body backward, as if going back under the fence. Finish in the Downward Dog position with your butt high. This exercise was performed by Pale Rider and named by Nomaha.

exercises.name: Twin Tower of Trust
exercises.description: Twin Tower of Trust Each PAX member will hold a brick in each hand, facing another PAX member (for example, with 20 guys total, 10 line up facing each other about 3-4 feet apart). When the Q calls the exercise, they will hold the bricks straight out at eye level, with no touching of bricks or partners'' bricks. The person on the end will bear crawl under everyone holding the bricks (this is where the trust comes in). When they reach the end, they will pick a side and hold their bricks up. The next person then goes, and this process continues. This exercise will last for 25-50 yards, depending on what the Q specifies.

exercises.name: Twinkle Toes
exercises.description: Twinkle Toes This exercise is an Al Gore (holding a squat with your arms and hands extended in front of you) while performing calf raises. It was originally created by F3 Knoxville''s Anchorman and is heavily used in F3 Knoxville JUCO.

exercises.name: Two Coupon Dip
exercises.description: A tricep exercise performed using two coupons (cinderblocks or similar) positioned parallel to each other at shoulder-width apart. Place one hand on top of each coupon and support your body weight with straight arms, legs extended in front or bent behind you. Lower your body by bending your elbows until you achieve full range of motion (upper arms parallel to ground or lower), then press back up to the starting position. The elevated hand position allows for deeper dips than floor-based variations, significantly increasing the challenge to your triceps, chest, and shoulders while engaging your core for stability

exercises.name: TYFBAF (Thank You For Being A Friend)
exercises.description: Play the Golden Girls theme song aka Thank You For Being a Friend. Every time the word friend is sung, you do a burpee. The rest of the time you should be doing side straddle hops or high knees. Can be done as a warmup.

exercises.name: UHaul
exercises.description: UHaul Begin in a standing position, holding a kettlebell or coupon with both hands at chest level. The exercise is performed in cadence with the following movements: Count 1, chest press straight out (parallel to the ground). Count 2, return the kettlebell to the chest. Count 3, perform an incline chest press at a 45-degree upward angle. Count 4, return the kettlebell to the chest.

exercises.name: Ultimate Football
exercises.description: Played like Ultimate Frisbee but with a football. The scoring team calls out an exercise for the entire PAX, but the ''losing'' team has to do double the amount. For example, if the exercise is ''10 burpees,'' the scoring team does 10 burpees, while the losing team does 20 burpees. If one team scores three times in a row, that team has to run a lap around the field in addition to their exercises. Play for an hour, usually around the holidays.

exercises.name: Ultimate Frisburpee
exercises.description: This game is similar to ultimate frisbee but includes penalty burpees. On the 1st turnover, all PAX do 5 burpees. On the 2nd turnover, it's 4 burpees; on the 3rd, 3 burpees; on the 4th turnover, 2 burpees; and on the 5th turnover, 1 burpee. If neither team has scored, the remainder of the point is played like regular ultimate frisbee. The burpee count resets after each point. You do not have to wait for all PAX to finish their burpees before beginning play (i.e., it is advantageous to finish your burpees quickly). The game is usually played for about 20 minutes and is a favorite in F3 Hampton Roads.

exercises.name: Uncle Mary
exercises.description: For the Mary, at the end of a workout, the Q leads an ab exercise, doing reps until a PAX calls \Uncle\". Then they lead the next ab exercise until another PAX calls \"Uncle\". Rinse and repeat until the end. "

exercises.name: Underdog
exercises.description: A supine pull-up is performed while hanging below a swing. Lying horizontally on the ground with your arms extended and hanging below the swing, lift your chest up toward the swing (a supine pull-up). This exercise can also be performed on a fence, bench, bars, or similar structures.

exercises.name: Up Dog
exercises.description: A conscious effort to catch breath and slow breathing so the following exercises can be performed with good attention to form and effort. Example: Q: Next exercise is.... Up Dog! Exhausted PAX: What's Up Dog? Q: Give us a 10 count, that's what's up.

exercises.name: Up downs
exercises.description: Up downs Chop your feet or do high knees. On the Q''s signal, drop your chest to the ground and bounce back up, then resume chopping your feet or doing high knees.

exercises.name: Up Straddle Hop
exercises.description: Side Straddle Hops performed using one leg. It's suggested to do 25-30 reps on each leg in cadence. I recently tore my calf and have been on injury reserve (IR) for a bit. Not wanting to miss our monthly convergence, I grabbed my kettlebell to use when the PAX were doing exercises I couldn't participate in and headed out to the beatdown. We circled up, and the first exercise called was SSH. Not wanting to put pressure on my torn calf, I started hopping on one leg in cadence while still doing the upper body motion. Apparently, it looked funny, as the PAX started laughing and then joined in. Forty-three men hopping on one leg and waving their arms would have looked awesome to any passersby! Doing 30 IC of those completely exhausted my right (good) leg, so I figured it might need a name. Let the PAX name it and do 25-30 IC with each leg. It burns! For good measure, the Q next called for 10 OYO burpees. I tried to do them one-legged, and after 2 burpees, I had absolutely no strength left in my good leg. So, doing burpees on one leg could be an interesting add-on to the exercise.

exercises.name: Upper Decker
exercises.description: Best used with sturdy picnic table or stairs. PAX will step up to the tabletop (two steps) when on top perform one air squat and step down. In reference to the movie MaGruber

exercises.name: Upright Row
exercises.description: Upright Row Grab a rock, concrete block (coupon), log, kettle bell, or a small 2.0. Use a close overhand grip at waist level and lift the object up to your chin. (Thanks to Clark in NoCo.)

exercises.name: Uptown 50
exercises.description: Uptown 50 There are 10 cones with the following exercises written on them: Burpee Broad Jump, Crab Walk, Partner Carry (P1), Partner Carry (P2), Alligator Crawl, Wheelbarrow (P1), Wheelbarrow (P2), Nur (Run Backwards), AYG (Sprint), and Bear Crawl. The cones are placed at the end zone and at the 50-yard line. PAX partner up and line up on the goal line. Start by doing 10 burpees, then travel to the 50-yard line using the method indicated on the first cone. Once you reach the 50-yard line, do 10 burpees and travel back using the method on the next cone. Repeat this process until you have used all the methods on the cones and completed your 100 burpees. Continue until audible swearing is heard or until time runs out.

exercises.name: V-Up
exercises.description: A challenging core exercise that targets the entire abdominal region. Lie flat on your back with arms extended overhead and legs straight. Simultaneously lift your legs and upper body off the ground, reaching your hands toward your toes to form a \V\" shape with your body at the top of the movement. Keep your legs and arms as straight as possible throughout. Lower back down with control to the starting position and repeat. This exercise builds serious core strength

exercises.name: V-Up Roll-Up
exercises.description: Performed in a 4-count cadence, this exercise is best known from P90X''s Ab Ripper. Start by lying flat with your arms extended above your head. Perform a full sit-up and touch your toes. As you return to the starting position, when your mid-back touches the ground, begin to raise your legs to a 45-degree angle and hold them there. Then, crunch up and touch your toes while keeping your legs in position.

exercises.name: Vacuum Cleaners
exercises.description: Vacuum Cleaners Partner work is done in the wheelbarrow position, ideally in a parking lot or a lined field. Starting at the first line, wheelbarrow to the second line and perform five Derkins. Reverse the wheelbarrow back to the first line for one rep. Flapjack after the agreed-upon number of reps have been completed.

exercises.name: Van Goghda
exercises.description: Van Goghda Think of lying down like Abe Vigoda. On your back with your legs spread, sit up while reaching as high as possible with one hand. Once you reach the peak, lower your hand forward and cross over to touch the opposite foot, then reverse the motion back down. Flip to the other side and repeat. For a harder variation, cross your legs.

exercises.name: Vertigos
exercises.description: Vertigos Run across a field or any stretch of road or earth, but with each step, rotate so that you are constantly spinning as you move forward (clockwise or counter-clockwise, your choice). If this doesn''t make you dizzy, then not much will.

exercises.name: Vicious circle
exercises.description: Vicious circle The PAX travel single-file around the bases of a baseball field. The last man (or last 2 men in larger groups) peels off, runs to the pitcher''s mound, performs 5 of an exercise, and then rejoins the PAX at the front. Repeat this for all PAX. Change the mode of travel and the exercises accordingly. For example: PAX bear crawl around the bases. The last man moseys to the pitcher''s mound for 5 Merkins and then returns to the front of the PAX. Or, PAX lunge walk around the bases. The last man moseys to the pitcher''s mound for 5 jump squats and then returns to the front.

exercises.name: Wake Up! (Chop Suey!)
exercises.description: Play System of a Down's \Chop Suey!\" Fast verses = High Knees / Fast Feet (Sprint in place). Slow melodic chorus (\"Father into your hands...\") = Slow

exercises.name: Walk Like An Egyptian
exercises.description: Walk Like An Egyptian The PAX line up single-file, about an arm''s length apart. In unison, the entire PAX lunges forward with their right leg and holds the lunge, while the last PAX jailbreaks to the front of the line. The entire PAX then recovers and lunges forward with their left leg and holds the lunge, while the last PAX jailbreaks to the front. This process continues until all PAX have cycled through at least once. To add some extra core work, have the PAX rotate their torso (e.g., rotate the chest right while in the right leg lunge) and hold the rotation throughout.

exercises.name: Walk The Dog
exercises.description: Perform any carry with a coupon.\n\nExample carries: coupon on one shoulder, suitcase (one hand to your side), food baby (front near abdomen), binocular (front near face), rifle (above head), child (front on one hip, one arm).

exercises.name: Walkerstrong
exercises.description: Walkerstrong The PAX circles up and performs 2 minutes of Imperial Walkers, with the last 30 seconds as AMRAP. The exercise is named in honor of Walker Phillips, 2.0 of NailPop in Lexington. A variation of this exercise involves each PAX taking a turn praying aloud with the group while continuing the Imperial Walkers until everyone has prayed.

exercises.name: Wall Conveyor
exercises.description: Wall Conveyor All PAX line up against the wall of a building in the Balls to the Wall (BTTW) position. The first PAX at one end of the line drops down from BTTW, runs to the end of the line, and then returns to the BTTW position. The next PAX follows, and this process repeats until the Q says to stop or you run out of wall space.

exercises.name: Wall Worms
exercises.description: Wall Worms Start in the Balls to the Wall (BTTW) position. Inch your way to the left or right by moving your hands on the ground and your feet on the wall. Alternatively, you can inch your hands out and your feet down the wall into a more horizontal position, and then move back up.

exercises.name: Walls of Jericho
exercises.description: Walls of Jericho It''s Biblical and it will hurt... just as Joshua was instructed to do. Walls of Jericho (WOJ) is usually performed around a large building. Pick 7 different exercises, complete 7 reps of each, and then run one lap around the building. Repeat this process for 7 laps. For an extra challenge, you can have the PAX shout on the last set to pay homage to the story.

exercises.name: Walmart Burpee
exercises.description: The burpee's discount-store cousin, where the fun is dialed back. Start by dropping into a squat like a normal burpee, but continue all the way down to your back, rolling onto your shoulder blades. Roll forward, and when your feet hit the ground, place your hands in front and jump back into a plank. The rest follows a standard burpee: perform a merkin, a thruster, and then jump with your hands overhead.

exercises.name: War Hammer
exercises.description: War Hammer This exercise is a combination of a World War II sit-up and four American Hammer reps at the top of the sit-up. It is similar to Captain Thor but does not include an ascending count.

exercises.name: Wave of Merkins
exercises.description: Wave of Merkins Circle up and get into plank position. The first person does one merkin, then the next person to the right does a merkin, creating a wave effect around the circle. When it reaches the first person again, they perform 2 merkins, and the wave continues. Repeat this pattern all the way up to 10 merkins and then back down. Stay in plank position the entire time.

exercises.name: We're Not Worthy
exercises.description: Everyone remembers Wayne and Garth from Wayne's World: \We're not worthy
aliases: we're not worthy.\" With arms extended outward

exercises.name: Webbicide
exercises.description: Webbicide A standard suicide but instead of touching each line the pax does an increasing number of merkins at each line. Run to the first line, do one merkin, run back to start, run to next line, do two merkins, and so on until you get to 10. A running form of the Jack Webb usually done on a large parking lot.

exercises.name: Weezy Jefferson
exercises.description: Weezy Jefferson Ab exercise performed lying on your back with hands under your bum and legs raised to a 90-degree angle. In cadence, lower your legs quickly toward the ground, stop them 6 inches above the ground without touching, and then move them back up to 90 degrees (like you''re \movin'' on up\" like George & Weezy). This exercise can also be used in place of foot-push-downs during a Guantanamo by pushing the 90-degree legs from the head side. (Thanks to DÃ¶ner Kebab in Richmond.)"

exercises.name: Welcome Party
exercises.description: Welcome Party Invite PAX to arrive 15 minutes before the workout. Any PAX can \host

exercises.name: Welsh Dragons
exercises.description: Welsh Dragons Stay in the plank position the entire time. Bear crawl forward 4 steps, then do 1 merkin, 1 plank jack, and tap the back of each shoulder once. Repeat, adding 1 rep to each exercise each timeâ€''bear crawl forward 4 steps, then do 2 merkins, 2 plank jacks, and 2 shoulder taps. Continue until you reach 10 reps of each exercise, have covered 30-50 feet, or until your arms give outâ€''whichever comes first.

exercises.name: Werewolf
exercises.description: Werewolf Start in the downward-facing dog position (high plank), transition to a low plank, then to upward-facing dog, and back to downward-facing dog in one smooth motion. On the return to downward-facing dog, the PAX should push through the shoulders to really feel the burn. It kind of looks like you are going from downward dog to howling at the moon. The first time the Smokehouse men did it, there was a full moon over Batesburg-Leesville.

exercises.name: Werkin
exercises.description: A push-up variation with hands positioned wider than shoulder-width apart, emphasizing the chest and outer pectorals. Start in a plank position with your body straight and arms extended wider than your shoulders. Lower your body down by bending your elbows, keeping your core tight and back flat, then press back up to the starting position. The wider hand placement shifts more emphasis to the chest muscles compared to a standard merkin. Can be counted as 'down one' or '1, 2, 3' and can be modified by dropping to your knees if needed. This variation builds upper body strength with a focus on chest development and shoulder stability

exercises.name: Whamo lunge walks
exercises.description: Whamo lunge walks Named after the toy company Wham-O. One pax throws a frisbee. All pax sprint in that direction until frisbee lands. When it lands, all PAX stop and then lunge-walk until they get to the frisbee. Can be modified with duck walk, crab walk, whatever.

exercises.name: Wheel of Animal Walk
exercises.description: Wheel of Animal Walk Essentially a Wheel of Merkin with Animal Walks between each stage. Find a parking lot 40-60 yards across, preferably with an incline (the steeper, the better). Begin at the bottom of the incline. Frog Hop across and do 10 merkins with both hands on the curb. Bear Crawl across and do 10 merkins with your left hand on the curb. Duck Walk across and do 10 merkins with your feet on the curb. Crab Walk across and do 10 merkins with your right hand on the curb. (North Augusta PAX LOVE this one.)

exercises.name: Wheel of Merkin
exercises.description: Grab a piece of curb and start in an incline plank position. Perform a set number of reps in each merkin position before rotating to the next. Begin with an incline merkin. Then, rotate 90 degrees clockwise until your body is parallel to the curb, with your left hand and foot on the curb and your right hand and foot off it. Perform a merkin, then rotate another 90 degrees and perform a decline merkin. Rotate 90 degrees again and repeat, this time with your right hand and foot on the curb and your left hand and foot off it.

exercises.name: When Animals Attack
exercises.description: Mark off an area (kill zone). All PAX must remain in the area at all times. One PAX (the attacker or \it\") starts in one corner
aliases: while all the other PAX start in the opposite corner. Assume the bear crawl position and stay there. The attacker begins to chase the other PAX throughout the kill zone (in bear crawl mode). Once contact is made with a PAX (\"tag\")

exercises.name: Wilt Chamberlains
exercises.description: Wilt Chamberlains Named for Wilt Chamberlain''s NBA record 100-point game. Space the cones 100 feet apart, the length of a basketball court. Line up at the first cone, do 100 LBCs, then run to the second cone and do 100 squats. Return to the first cone to do 100 flutter kicks (each leg), and then finish at the second cone with 100 lunges (50 each leg). This is a nice ab and lower body routine.

exercises.name: Windmill
exercises.description: Standing windmill exercise: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, one arm straight up, and the other hand inside the opposite leg. Rotate torso and push hips back. Eyes should follow the back hand. Variations: Abe Vagoda = Slow 4-count

exercises.name: Windshield Wipers
exercises.description: Windshield Wipers This is a 10-count exercise. Start on your six with legs together at 90 degree angle. First, move both legs together to the left, till they are 6 inches from the ground. Then go to the right till they are six inches from the ground all the way to the right. Finally, go back to the middle to end the 10-count. Rinse and repeat.

exercises.name: Wing Nut
exercises.description: Wing Nut Done in cadence. Start with sitting on a picnic table with your feet under the bench, lean back to approximately 15 degrees with your back and neck straight. With your fists touching in front of your chest and elbows pointing parallel with your shoulders, rotate your waist so that each elbow touches the tabletop. Complete in a 4-count cadence.

exercises.name: Wojo Squats
exercises.description: Named after Duke basketball player Steve Wojciechowski, who would slap the floor, these are squats where you slap the pavement at the bottom of each rep, just like an overly energetic Duke basketball player.

exercises.name: Wood Chopper
exercises.description: Grab a sandbag (for the added instability) or a paver. Start in a crouch, twisted to the left, with the sandbag outside your left shin (1). Swing the sandbag up across your body to the right to a standing press position, with the weight outside your right shoulder (2). Swing back down to the left to the starting position (3). Back up (4). Swing vigorously enough to require deceleration at both the top and bottom really go for it, as if you were chopping wood. Perform this for a 20-count in cadence. Switch sides to start on the right and repeat. Intermingle with another exercise for recovery, and complete 3 sets for a true crowd-pleaser. Expect some serious soreness from hips to armpits. Disclaimer: Maintain good form and straight backs! Those with bad backs or unfit FNGs should modify to jumpies with the weight.

exercises.name: Wooly Worm aka Inch Worm.
exercises.description: Wooly Worm aka Inch Worm. Place your feet on the swing seat while in a plank position. Bring your knees to your chest, pulling the swing with your feet for a suspended reverse crunch (inchworm movement). Introduced at SVU during the Wooly Worm Festival.

exercises.name: World War I Sit-up
exercises.description: World War I sit-up Starting on your six with arms fully extended above your head and legs straight, raise your upper body while keeping your arms straight above your head until you are upright. Then, bring your arms as far forward as possible, trying to touch your toes. Return to the starting position, flat on the ground.

exercises.name: World War I Snow Angel
exercises.description: World War I Snow Angel (Only to be called in the snow) Perform the same motion as a WW1 sit-up, but when you are in the extended position on your back, make motions to create a snow angel on counts 3 and 4.

exercises.name: World War II Sit-up
exercises.description: Done in cadence. Start on your back with knees bent, both arms extended above your head on the ground. On count one, Pax will move arms to bent knees while sitting up. On count two, PAX will return to the starting position with arms extended over the head on the ground. Counts 3 and 4 will repeat Counts 1 and 2. That is one rep.

exercises.name: World War III Sit-up
exercises.description: The WW3 sit-up is a one-coupon exercise where, starting in the down position, you will execute a chest-press with the coupon and then bring the coupon to your knees on the up. Then execute a final shoulder press before the down, rinse and repeat.

exercises.name: Worst Merkin Ever
exercises.description: Worst Merkin Ever Three merkins performed in succession with no break in form: 1 Wide Arm Merkin, 1 Regular Merkin, and 1 Diamond Merkin = 1 Worst Merkin Ever.

exercises.name: Worst Merkin Ever Crawl
exercises.description: WMD Crawl A Bear Crawl executed by performing 5 Werkins, then crawling 10 steps with hands set out wide, followed by 5 Regular Merkins and crawling 10 steps with hands in a normal position, and finishing with 5 Diamond Merkins and crawling 10 steps with hands set close. Rinse and repeat until the desired distance has been traveled.

exercises.name: Worst Worst Merkin Ever
exercises.description: That''s right, it can ALWAYS be worse! Done in cadence: start with a Crucible Merkin on 1, a Wercan on 2, a regular Merkin on 3, and a Diamond Merkin on 4. Do ten of these, and you''ll agree it''s the worst of the worst.

exercises.name: Wounded Bear Crawl
exercises.description: Wounded Bear Crawl Using a curb, crawl along it with one arm and one leg on the curb and the other arm and leg on the asphalt or concrete next to it. You''ll feel like a wounded animal.

exercises.name: X Gon' Give It To Ya
exercises.description: Play DMX's \X Gon' Give It To Ya\" On your six to perform \"X-Factors\": arms and legs wide forming an \"X\". Crunch up touching opposite hand to opposite foot. Keep the rhythm aggressive. "

exercises.name: X-Cross Sit-up
exercises.description: Performed in slow cadence. Starting position: Lie on your back with your legs and arms extended in an ''X'' position. On count 1, simultaneously lift your left leg and right arm to meet above your core, keeping both as straight as possible while touching your right hand to your left leg. Return to the starting ''X'' position. On count 2, switch to lifting your right leg and left arm, again keeping both as straight as possible while touching your left hand to your right leg above your core. Return to the starting ''X'' position. On count 3, execute a knee-up by lifting your shoulders and legs off the ground and touching your toes as you bring your knees up, with only your lower back in contact with the ground. On count 4, extend your arms and legs and return to the starting ''X'' position. That completes one rep.

exercises.name: X-Factor
exercises.description: X-Factor Start on your back with your arms and legs spread out to resemble an X. Start the move with a V-up, with your right arm touching your left shin, then lower down and perform another V-up with your left arm touching your right shin. The opposite arm and leg stay down. That''s one rep. For a bonus, raise your arms and legs 6 inches off the ground and start the move from there, keeping the down arm and leg at 6 inches while performing the V-up.

exercises.name: Xs and Os
exercises.description: Lie down on your six, with your arms and legs spread out into an X position. This is the 'X' in Xs and Os. Pull your arms and legs toward your body, curling up into a ball when the Q calls out 'O'. This is the 'O' in Xs and Os. Then move back into the X position when the Q calls out 'X' again.

exercises.name: XYs
exercises.description: XYs 4-count hybrid of Rosalita with a Flutter Kick: Start with legs at a 45-degree angle. Counts 1 and 2 are the Rosalita, with Counts 3 and 4 going directly into the Flutter Kick.

exercises.name: Yoke Walk
exercises.description: Yoke Walk Many different variations with this one, but you''ll need something heavy to carry across your shoulders and behind your neck. Can involve a heavy bar for a single pax member or pax can team up with heavy objects like logs.

exercises.name: Yul Brynner
exercises.description: Yul Brynner was an actor in the 1960s movie The Magnificent Seven . The Magnificent Seven Merkins involve progressing through seven different types of merkins without stopping or holding plank between each one: Carolina Dry Dock, Wide Merkin, Normal Merkin, Ranger Merkin, Diamond Merkin, Crucible Merkin, and Chuck Norris Merkin. Rinse and repeat on the Q''s call.

exercises.name: Yurpee
exercises.description: A Burpee variation in honor of the Ruskie (Yuri Verkhoshansky), who developed plyometrics to train Olympians: The merkin at the bottom of the burpee is a clap-merkin, and on the up portion, there are two tuck jumps per burpee. Basically, it's a Clurpee with two tuck jumps.

exercises.name: Yves Poll
exercises.description: Yves Poll (pronounced \eves pole\" in English) is a term for running backwards or backpedaling. He was a French marathon runner who ran backwards all the time. Submitted by SkyHook in Houston
aliases: whose father-in-law was an ultramarathoner and one of Yves'' running buddies."

exercises.name: Zebra Butt-Kicks
exercises.description: Ever seen a zebra launch another Savannah beast into the sky? If so, it was with that butt kick that they do so perfectly. Virtually no exercises wear out our glutes in F3. The zebra kick is all about the gluteus maximus. From plank, bend your knee to 90 degrees and kick skyward. High reps, imagine your heel busting up the chops of a lioness.

exercises.name: Zig-Zag Hops
exercises.description: Find a beam, log, or other object even a PAX member or two (who has signed a disclaimer) to jump over. Start on one side and hop over in a zig-zag fashion from one end to the other. Added because we need something that begins with a Z.

exercises.name: Zombie Crawl
exercises.description: Start in a plank position and crawl with both legs dragging behind. For an added challenge, try a Zombie Crawl on an incline.

exercises.name: Zombie Crunch
exercises.description: LBC with both legs bent/laid to one side (like a dead zombie). Then repeat for other side.

exercises.name: Zombie Merkins
exercises.description: A challenging push-up variation. With your hands shoulder-width apart, lower your body until your chest touches the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides, then press back up to the starting position. At the top of the Merkin, raise one hand horizontally forward, then back down to the ground. At the top of the next Merkin

exercises.name: Zombie Walk
exercises.description: A lunge walk over a long distance that causes the PAX to groan and stumble like the Walking Dead.

exercises.name: Mosey
exercises.description: A light jog from one location to another. The standard F3 way to transition between stations during a beatdown. Not a sprint — just an easy, conversational-pace jog.

exercises.name: Abe Vigoda
exercises.description: Classic F3 warm-up exercise. Feet shoulder-width apart, bend at the waist and touch the ground, rise up on your toes, then squat. Repeat in cadence. Named after the long-lived actor as a nod to aging gracefully.

exercises.name: Cherry Picker
exercises.description: Standing stretch and warm-up. Feet shoulder-width apart, reach down and touch the ground between your feet (count 1), outside your left foot (count 2), outside your right foot (count 3), then stand back up (count 4). Done in cadence.

exercises.name: Tappy Taps
exercises.description: Side-to-side warmup shuffle. Step laterally to the right, tapping the left foot next to the right foot (count 1-2), then step left and tap the right foot next to the left (count 3-4). Stay low and keep a quick tempo.

exercises.name: Toy Soldier
exercises.description: High-kick march. Walk forward kicking each straight leg up to waist height (or higher) while reaching the opposite hand to meet the foot. Engages hamstrings and core.

exercises.name: Baby Arm Circles
exercises.description: Small arm circles for shoulder warm-up. Arms extended out to the sides, make small tight circles forward for the first count, then reverse direction. A staple of the F3 Warm-O-Rama.

exercises.name: Iron Mikes
exercises.description: Alternating jump lunges. Start in a lunge position, jump explosively and switch legs in the air, landing softly in a lunge with the opposite leg forward. Keep your torso upright and land with control.

exercises.name: Box Jump
exercises.description: Explosive plyometric jump onto a box, bench, curb, or other elevated surface. Stand in front of the surface, bend knees and swing arms back, then explode upward landing with both feet on top. Step down carefully and repeat.

exercises.name: Low Slow Flutter
exercises.description: Flutter kicks performed at a deliberately slow tempo with legs kept very close to the ground. On your six, both legs raised 6 inches, alternate kicking up and down in a slow controlled cadence. Burns more than you think.

exercises.name: Plank Hold
exercises.description: Static plank hold in the up position (high plank) or down position (low plank / forearm plank). Core stability exercise used as rest between sets or as an active hold during beatdowns.

exercises.name: Sprint
exercises.description: An all-out run at maximum effort to a given distance or landmark. Unlike a mosey, sprints are done at full speed. Common in beatdowns as a finisher or between exercise sets.

exercises.name: Indian Run
exercises.description: Group running drill. PAX form a single-file line jogging together. The last man in line sprints to the front, then the new last man sprints to the front, and so on. Continues until Q calls it.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
exercises.description: Upper body pulling exercise on a bar or rings. Start hanging with arms fully extended, pull body up until chin clears the bar, lower back down with control. Palms facing away from you (overhand grip).

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.description: Upper body pulling exercise on a bar. Like a pull-up but with palms facing toward you (underhand/supinated grip). Emphasizes the biceps slightly more than standard pull-ups.

exercises.name: Calf Raise
exercises.description: Standing calf raise. Rise up on the balls of your feet, then lower back down. Can be done on flat ground or with heels hanging off a curb or step for greater range of motion.

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description: Kneel on one knee (lunge position), lean hips forward to stretch the hip flexor of the rear leg. Hold for several counts then switch legs. Common warm-up and cool-down stretch in F3.

exercises.name: ARod's
exercises.description: Combination of a J-Lo and a Pickle Pounder. On your six, combine both movements in sequence.

exercises.name: Bear Crawl 360
exercises.description: Perform a Bear Crawl while rotating 360 degrees the entire time. Can be performed clockwise or counter-clockwise.

exercises.name: Cusack
exercises.description: Exercises completed while holding or carrying a CMU or block overhead, usually in conjunction with running or walking. Named after John Cusack holding the boom box in "Say Anything."

exercises.name: Dominoes
exercises.description: PAX side-plank on a row of curbs. Starting at one end, the first PAX rotates to plank and does Merkins, then holds while the next PAX rotates down, cascading down the line like falling dominoes.

exercises.name: Eagle Wings
exercises.description: A modified Bat Wings where bricks or coupons are used in each hand. Arms out to sides, make slow circles forward then reverse.

exercises.name: FrankenSteinl
exercises.description: The Steinl, only the routine is performed while wearing a ruck sack or vest.

exercises.name: Hallelujahs
exercises.description: Have hands raised during any or all exercises. Creates a major burn by removing the ability to rest arms at your sides. Can be applied to squats, lunges, etc.

exercises.name: Knerkin
exercises.description: A traditional Merkin performed on the knuckles instead of the open palm. Aka the "Chuck Norris." Keeps wrists neutral and builds grip strength.

exercises.name: Russian Twist
exercises.description: On your six in a partial sit-up position with heels just off the ground, twist at the waist so both hands held together almost touch the ground beside your waist, alternating in cadence from side to side. Often done with a coupon.

exercises.name: Sally's Cousin
exercises.description: Play 'Flower' by Moby. Instead of starting in the merkin position, begin in a downward squat and hold. Each time 'Bring Sally Up' is sung, stand up; 'Bring Sally Down' returns you to the squat. Your quads will be destroyed.

exercises.name: Shawshank
exercises.description: A standing "Have a Nice Day." Done at the end of a workout on a rainy day: stand up, spread arms wide, tilt head back, and look up into the rain like Andy Dufresne escaping Shawshank prison.

exercises.name: Tennessee Rocking Chair
exercises.description: Four-count cadence warmup. Start standing with arms straight out front. (1) Bend at the waist and touch toes. (2) Bend knees to squat, torso comes back to vertical. (3) Straighten knees back to toe-touch position. (4) Return to start.

exercises.name: The Chinook
exercises.description: Overhead arm circles performed so you resemble the motion of a Chinook helicopter rotor. Arms go in full overhead circles as a shoulder warmup done in cadence.

exercises.name: Tie Fighters
exercises.description: Perform Baby Arm Circles (BACs) while holding a lunge position. Do forward BACs with the right leg forward, then backward BACs with the left leg forward. Named for the wing-arm motion resembling TIE Fighter ships. Good warm-up routine.

exercises.name: Turn and Bounce
exercises.description: Feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms extended palms up. Twist torso left and bounce three times (counts 1-3), return to center (count 4 = ONE). Flapjack: twist right, bounce 1-2-3, return to center (TWO). Dynamic torso warmup.

exercises.name: Water Wheel
exercises.description: Slow cadence warmup. Stand with feet wide and toes forward. (1) Bend right leg (left stays straight) and touch right foot. (2) Straighten right leg to center. (3) Bend left leg (right stays straight) and touch left foot. (4) Straighten to center. A lateral hip hinge warmup.

exercises.name: Weekend at Bernie's
exercises.description: Partner drag exercise where one partner is a 'casualty' and the other drags them down the field. Any drag style works. Named for the movie where characters haul around a lifeless body.

exercises.name: Wilson's Wife
exercises.description: Inverted military press with your feet up on your partner's shoulders — essentially a BTTW (handstand) without a wall. Partner serves as your wall.

exercises.name: Star Jack
exercises.description: A jumping jack variation where arms go out wide to the sides making a star shape at the top. Sometimes performed with a wider leg spread. Done in cadence as a warmup.

exercises.name: Sun Gods
exercises.description: Rotating shoulder warmup. Arms extended out to sides, make large full circles forward, then reverse. Can be done as "Little Sun Gods" (small tight circles) or "Big Sun Gods" (large circles). Done in cadence.

exercises.name: Don Quixotes
exercises.description: Aka Windmill. Feet wider than shoulder-width apart, arms straight out to the sides. With legs kept straight, bend at the waist and touch your opposite hand to your opposite toe, alternating sides in cadence.

exercises.name: Steinl
exercises.description: A plank-based routine combining various plank positions and transitions. Referenced as the base exercise for FrankenSteinl (the Steinl performed while wearing a ruck sack).

exercises.name: Michael Phelps
exercises.description: Shoulder and chest warmup. Cross arms in front of your body and swing them back wide, slapping your own back, then bring back to front. Mimics a swimmer warming up. Done in cadence as part of the Warm-O-Rama.

exercises.name: Hello Dolly
exercises.description: On your six, legs raised about 6 inches off the ground, spread legs wide apart then bring them back together. Similar to Dolly but commonly used interchangeably. Classic F3 Mary exercise.

exercises.name: Motivators
exercises.description: Starting from 7 (or any number), perform that many burpees then that many squats. Count down to 1. Example: 7 burpees + 7 squats, 6 + 6, ... 1 + 1. A classic F3 beatdown finisher.

exercises.name: Chest Press
exercises.description: Lie on your six, coupon held at chest level. Press the coupon straight up until arms are fully extended, then lower back to chest. Done in cadence. Also called Floor Press when performed on the ground.
